scenario: A purchase-order Form has an editable "Line Items" section where the user adds / removes rows. Each row needs four fields: SKU, Quantity, Unit Price, Notes. The team needs to choose the right Forms element and configure its "row schema."
question: How is an editable repeating-row "table" configured in Camunda Forms?

options.text: md -- the Table element is a read-only display component for presenting existing data as a formatted table. Its Data source is expression-only per :14, meaning it renders data from a FEEL expression but does not support user editing, adding rows, or removing rows. Table has no editable cells and no add-row or remove-row controls. For user-editable repeating data where users add and remove entries, the Dynamic List element is the correct form component toing summaries, not for capturing line-item input.

Configure Dynamic List by defining each column or field using a FEEL expression that dynamically returns the column structure at runtime based on process variables -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Dynamic List child field definitions are configured statically in the form schema as form component definitions. The structure of each repeating row -- which fields appear, their types, labels, and validation rules -- is fixed in the form JSON at design time. The Dynamic List manages runtime flexibility in the number of rows users can add and remove; the per-row field structure is static and does not change based on runtime FEEL expressions.

md:7-25. , lineItems) per :14. Inside the dynamic list, place each per-row form field (Textfield for SKU, Number for Quantity & Unit Price, Textarea for Notes) with its own Key relative to the row item (sku, quantity, unitPrice, notes). md:72-92.

Camunda Forms have no capability for user-editable repeating rows -- the platform cannot render forms where users add new rows or remove existing rows from a dynamic list of entries -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. The Dynamic List element is specifically designed and documented for this exact use case. It renders a repeating section where users can add new instances of the defined field template and remove existing entries. The resulting data is automatically serialised as a list of objects in the bound process variable. Dynamic List is a standard element in the Camunda Forms element library.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda Forms has two distinct elements for tabular layouts: Dynamic list (editable) -- forms-element-library-dynamiclist.md -- repeating-row container with Path binding, Default number of items, Allow add/delete items per :13-19. Each child form field inside the dynamic list becomes a per-row input; the child's Key is relative to the row-item object. Table (read-only) -- forms-element-library-table.md -- displays tabular data from a FEEL Data source; no per-cell input. For "purchase order with add/remove line items," Dynamic List is the canonical choice. The submitted variable shape is lineItems = [{sku, quantity, unitPrice, notes}, ...] per the dynamic-list data-binding example at forms-config-data-binding.md:72-92.

b.text: Incorrect. Table element is read-only display.

c.text: Correct. Child fields are statically declared in the schema.

d.text: Incorrect. Dynamic List supports repeating rows.

explanation: Child fields are statically declared in the schema.
